activities_projects.title: Critical Analysis Essay
activities_projects.objective: Develop the ability to critically analyze the story
steps: Analyze the story’s central conflict between Kezia and her father
Discuss how the author portrays authority and childhood emotions
Write a 150–200 word essay evaluating the narrative techniques used
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances critical thinking, analytical writing, and literary interpretation
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis, clarity, and use of textual evidence (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Character Study
activities_projects.objective: Compare Kezia with a character from another story
steps: Identify another young girl from a different story or chapter
Compare her experiences, behavior, and relationship with authority
Present your comparison in a structured table or essay
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops comparative thinking and understanding of character traits
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, depth of comparison, and examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Perspective Writing
activities_projects.objective: Explore alternative perspectives in the story
steps: Rewrite a scene from the father’s point of view
Include his thoughts and feelings during the pin-cushion incident or nightmare
Write at least 10 sentences reflecting his perspective
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Encourages empathy, analytical skills, and creative writing
activities_projects.evaluation: Consistency with character traits and depth of insight (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Symbolism and Theme Analysis
steps: Identify symbols such as the pin-cushion, the father’s gestures, or the nightmare
Explain what each symbol represents in 2–3 sentences
Connect symbols to the story’s larger themes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances literary interpretation and thematic understanding
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, depth of explanation, and textual support (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Advanced Vocabulary and Contextual Usage
steps: Select 10 challenging words from the chapter
Write dictionary meanings, synonyms, antonyms, and contextual usage in the story
Construct original sentences showing nuanced meaning
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Improves advanced vocabulary and language precision
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct usage, creativity, and understanding of nuance (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Literary Device Exploration
activities_projects.objective: Identify and analyze literary devices used by the author
steps: Find examples of metaphor, simile, imagery, and dialogue
Explain their effect on the story’s mood and characterization
Present examples in a tabular form with 2–3 sentences each
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Strengthens understanding of literary techniques and their impact
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, depth, and clarity of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Predictive Writing
steps: Imagine what Kezia’s relationship with her father will be like 5 years later
Write a 10–12 sentence narrative predicting future interactions and growth
Justify your predictions using clues from the story
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, plausibility, and textual support (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Interpersonal Analysis Debate
activities_projects.objective: Critically examine Kezia’s and her father’s behavior through discussion
steps: Form groups and debate whether the father’s strictness was justified
Use examples from the text to support arguments
Write a reflective paragraph summarizing your personal stance after the debate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances critical thinking, discussion, and reasoning skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments and reflection (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Symbolic Scene Illustration
activities_projects.objective: Express understanding of key scenes creatively
steps: Select a scene with symbolic significance (e.g., the nightmare or the pin-cushion incident)
Draw it creatively, highlighting symbolic elements
Write a 3–4 sentence explanation of symbolism depicted
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, clarity, and symbolism understanding (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Intertextual Connection Writing
activities_projects.objective: Relate the story to real-life experiences or other literature
steps: Identify a real-life situation or another story where parent–child relationships are explored
Write a 120–150 word piece connecting it to Kezia’s experience
Highlight similarities and differences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops analytical thinking, connection-making, and writing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of insight, relevance, and clarity (10 marks)
